summary.zh: “列出这个文件夹里大于 1 GB 的文件,从大到小排序,把列表存到 big-files.txt。”
summary.en: "List the files larger than 1 GB in this folder, biggest first, and save the list to big-files.txt."

beats.thought: 列出这个文件夹里大于 1 GB 的文件,从大到小排序,把列表存到 big-files.txt。想清楚再动手。

title.zh: 模型决定:先看看有什么

summary.zh: 它没有“决定”。它逐字预测,而预测出的这串字,恰好是一条列目录的命令。
summary.en: It didn't "decide." It predicted tokens one by one — and the tokens it predicted happen to form a list-the-folder command.

beats.thought: I'll think this through before acting: "this folder" likely means recursively within the current dir, and the threshold is 1 GB. First, let me see what's actually here.

arguments.command: pwd && echo "---" && ls -la && echo "--- df for context ---" && df -h .

title.en: It is guessing the next token

body.zh: 模型一次只做一件事:根据目前的文字,预测下一个最可能的 token。把这些 token 接起来,就成了你看到的“它决定去看文件夹”。它从头到尾没有“意图”,只有概率。
body.en: The model does exactly one thing at a time: given the text so far, predict the most likely next token. String those tokens together and you get what looks like "it decided to inspect the folder." There is no intent underneath — only probability.

title.zh: 分词 → 概率 → 采样 → 拼成调用

body.zh: 你的话先被切成 token;每一步,模型给整个词表打分(概率);采样挑一个;重复,直到拼出一段符合工具格式的 JSON。“调用工具”不是一个特殊动作,只是恰好长这样的一串文字。
body.en: Your words are split into tokens; at each step the model scores the whole vocabulary; sampling picks one; repeat until the tokens spell a tool-shaped piece of JSON. "Calling a tool" isn't a special action — it's just text that happens to look like this.

label.zh: 最长的条 = 模型觉得最可能的下一个 token
label.en: the longest bar = the model's most likely next token

title.zh: 最底层:真实的数字(你可以亲手摸)
title.en: The bottom: the real numbers (you can touch them)

body.zh: 真实 token id、带小数的 logprob、模型吐出的原始命令,以及一条约定:你的运行时看到符合这个格式的字符串,就把它当成一次工具调用去执行。拖动下面的温度,就能当场重塑这同一组分数;想用你自己的文字试,就在你的设备上跑这个模型。
body.en: Real token ids, decimal logprobs, the literal emitted command, and one convention: when your runtime sees a string in this shape, it treats it as a tool call and runs it. Drag the temperature below to reshape these same scores; or run the model on your own device to try your own words.

zoom.seedContext: I'm cleaning up a folder full of large files. To list them with their sizes, the shell command is: ls

bridge.zh: 可这串文字怎么就“真的列出了文件”?
bridge.en: But how does this text actually list the files?

title.en: The runtime really lists the folder

summary.zh: 不是模型在翻你的硬盘。是你的运行时拿着那串命令,真的执行,真的读到这些文件和大小。
summary.en: The model didn't read your disk. Your runtime took that command string, really executed it, and really saw these files and sizes.

beats.observation:
$ ls -la
-rw-r--r--  196   .gitignore
-rw-r--r--  816   README.md
-rw-------  356515840    app.log
-rw-------  3355443200   archive-old.tar
-rw-------  12582912     cover-photo.jpg
-rw-------  1887436800   dataset-2024.zip
-rw-------  1153433600   db-dump.sql
-rw-------  2516582400   video-master.mov

title.en: The model never touches your computer

body.zh: 模型只生成了一串文字。是运行时真的去跑 `ls`、读到每个文件的字节数、把这段真实输出递回去。这一步是整个故事里“token → 真实世界”的接缝。
body.en: The model only produced a string. It's the runtime that actually runs `ls`, reads each file's byte count, and hands that real output back. This step is the seam where "tokens" meet "the real world."

bridge.zh: 看到这些文件和大小后,模型会怎么挑出大文件?
bridge.en: Now that it has the files and sizes, how does it pick out the big ones?

title.zh: 模型筛出大文件 → 写进文件
title.en: The model filters the big files → writes the list

summary.zh: 同样的机制:它把目录列表也当作文字读进去,再预测出一段“过滤 + 排序 + 写文件”的命令。
summary.en: Same mechanism: it reads the directory listing as more text, then predicts a string that happens to be a filter-sort-and-write command.

beats.thought: The folder is flat — six files, four over 1 GiB. I'll still run a recursive find to be safe, exclude big-files.txt itself, filter to > 1 GiB, sort largest-first, and tee the result into big-files.txt.

arguments.command: find . -type f -not -path './.git/*' -not -name 'big-files.txt' -size +1073741824c -exec stat -f '%z %N' {} \; | sort -rn -k1,1 | awk '{ b=$1; $1=""; sub(/^ /,""); gib=b/1073741824; gb=b/1000000000; printf "%-22s %15d bytes  %6.2f GiB  %6.2f GB\n", $0, b, gib, gb }' | tee big-files.txt
arguments.description: Find files >1GiB, sort desc, write to big-files.txt

title.zh: 还是在猜 token —— 只是上下文变长了
title.en: Still guessing tokens — just with more context

body.zh: 和上一关“去看文件夹”是完全相同的机制。唯一的区别是,现在的上下文里多了那段目录列表。模型据此预测出的下一串 token,恰好是一条带 `find`、`sort`、阈值和重定向的 shell 管道。“筛选”不是模型的特殊能力,只是又一串恰好有用的文字。
body.en: This is the exact same machinery as the "inspect the folder" beat. The only difference is that the directory listing is now part of the context. The next tokens it predicts from it happen to be a shell pipeline with `find`, `sort`, a threshold, and a redirect. "Filtering" isn't a special skill — it's just another string that happens to be useful.

bridge.zh: 这条管道跑完,会得到什么?
bridge.en: And when that pipeline runs, what comes out?

title.zh: 运行时真的筛 + 真的写盘
title.en: The runtime really filters + really writes

summary.zh: 又一次:模型只产生了“怎么筛、写到哪”的文字,真正读遍文件、排序、把 big-files.txt 落到你硬盘上的,是运行时。
summary.en: Again: the model only produced the text of "how to filter and where to write"; the runtime is what actually walks the files, sorts them, and drops big-files.txt onto your disk.

beats.observation:
./archive-old.tar           3355443200 bytes    3.12 GiB    3.36 GB
./video-master.mov          2516582400 bytes    2.34 GiB    2.52 GB
./dataset-2024.zip          1887436800 bytes    1.76 GiB    1.89 GB
./db-dump.sql               1153433600 bytes    1.07 GiB    1.15 GB

bridge.zh: 四个大文件,从大到小,真的写进了 big-files.txt。
bridge.en: Four big files, largest first, really written to big-files.txt.

title.zh: 收工 —— 没有魔法,只有约定

summary.zh: 一个只会预测 token 的模型,靠“写文字 + 运行时执行”这几条约定,真的翻了你的文件夹、筛出大文件、写好了清单。
summary.en: A model that only predicts tokens, via a few conventions — emit text, let the runtime execute — really walked your folder, found the big files, and wrote the list.

beats.thought: Done. Found 4 files over 1 GB and wrote them to big-files.txt, largest first: archive-old.tar (3.36 GB), video-master.mov (2.52 GB), dataset-2024.zip (1.89 GB), db-dump.sql (1.15 GB). The decoys (app.log 357 MB, cover-photo.jpg 13 MB) were correctly left out.
